author: yorickdowne
content: “3,533 epochs is the realistic upper bound on the WS period” and that gives them 113,056 blocks.

author: yorickdowne
content:
Because: “With `SAFETY_DECAY = 10` and a validator set above the churn-limit floor (`N > 4 · 65536`), `compute_weak_subjectivity_period` reduces to ~3,533 epochs independent of `N`, stake, or balance distribution; below the floor it scales linearly with `N`.”

Independent of size of validator set.

author: yorickdowne
content: However. Ok. There is a push to get the validator set below that churn limit floor, to 128,000. What is WSP then?

author: arnetheduck
arnetheduck
content:
these two things are separate: 
* wsp was chosen as _retention period_ so that anyone with a state from within the WSP would be guaranteed to be able to sync from the network -> the network *must* carry blocks to make this happen
* reorg depth should be based on finality, not WSP - ie when there's non-finality for N epochs (3:ish weeks at some point but this should be computed based on the latest inactivity leak formula etc), the chain dies due to all validators being expelled by the inactivity leak -> we can go home because all validators are gone -> this is how deep of a reorg the EL should support

author: arnetheduck
content: there's one more point: the execution client must store execution payloads for at least as long as the cl is required to supply them to the networks, or cl:s that don't have their own payload storage will break

author: s1na
content: Some CLs indeed rely on EL for the payload https://discord.com/channels/595666850260713488/598292067260825641/1501934136540139580
